title: alnus fruticosa f. rufinervis
intro: Alnus fruticosa f. rufinervis, also known as the red-veined alder or alder tree, is a plant species that belongs to the alder family, characterized by its distinctive red-veined leaves. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Alnus fruticosa f. rufinervis include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and providing antioxidant effects, which can help protect against cell damage and promote overall well-being. The herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions and promote relaxation and calmness.
